THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE AND VAN WERT COUNTY EMERGENCY MANAGEMENT CONFIRMED THAT A WEAK EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN EASTERN VAN WERT COUNTY. THE TORNADO HEAVILY DAMAGED SEVERAL LARGE TREES...WITH 23 STRUCTURES RECEIVING DAMAGE TO SOME DEGREE INCLUDING BUILDINGS ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF DELPHOS. THE TORNADO HAD A PATH LENGTH OF 5 MILES AND WAS AT MOST 80 YARDS WIDE. MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 86 TO 93 MPH.

THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE CONFIRMED AN EF2 TORNADO IN FULTON COUNTY OHIO. THE TORNADO BEGAN ON HIGHWAY 109 JUST NORTH OF COUNTY ROAD A...CONTINUED TO THE NE THROUGH MAUMEE STATE FOREST AND THEN ENTERED LUCAS COUNTY OHIO. THE TORNADO HAD A PATH LENGTH OF 7.5 MILES AND WAS HALF A MILE WIDE AT THE WIDEST POINT. MULTIPLE HOUSES AND STRUCTURES WERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED. NUMEROUS TREES WERE UPROOTED...STRIPPED OR DAMAGED. MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 111 TO 135 MPH.
BRANCHES AND POWER LINES DOWN...HOUSE DAMAGE FROM POWER LINES...POSSIBLE TORNADO

ROOF OFF OF HOUSE IN STEVENSVILLE.
POLE BARN DESTROYED. 2 BY 4S BLOWN INTO A HOUSE. WINDOWS BLOWN OUT.

THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE CONFIRMED AN EF2 TORNADO IN BERRIEN COUNTY MICHIGAN. THE TORNADO BEGAN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF STEVENSVILLE-BARODA RD AND MARRS RD AND CONTINUED ENE ENDING NEAR SCOTTDALE RD BETWEEN LINCO AND ROCK WEED RD. THE TORNADO HAD A PATH LENGTH OF 3.4 MILES AND AT ITS WIDEST POINT WAS 300 YARDS. NUMEROUS STRUCTURES RECEIVED DAMAGE...INCLUDING TWO HOUSES THAT WERE DEROOFED. NUMEROUS TREES WERE UPROOTED AND DAMAGED. MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 111 TO 135 MPH.

THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE CONFIRMED AN EF1 TORNADO IN BERRIEN COUNTY MICHIGAN. THE TORNADO BEGAN ALONG LINCO ROAD JUST EAST OF SCOTTDALE ROAD AND SOUTHEAST OF THE END POINT OF THE FIRST TORNADO. THIS TORNADO HAD A PATH LENGTH OF 1.5 MILES AND A WIDTH OF 50 YARDS. TREES AND STRUCTURES RECEIVED DAMAGE ALONG THIS PATH...INCLUDING A POLE BARN THAT WAS DESTROYED. MAXIMUM WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 86 TO 110 MPH.

THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE CONFIRMED AN EF2 TORNADO IN CASS COUNTY MICHIGAN. THE TORNADO BEGAN HALF A MILE SOUTHWEST OF SOUTHWESTERN MICHIGAN COLLEGE NEAR DOWAGIAC AND CONTINUED TO THE ESE ENDING NEAR ROUTE 62 JUST SOUTH OF CASS STREET. THE TORNADO HAD A PATH LENGTH OF 2.75 MILES. NUMEROUS HOUSES WERE DAMAGED IN THE POKAGAN SUBDIVISION AND EXTENSIVE TREE DAMAGE WAS RECEIVED ALONG THE PATH. MAXIMUM WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 111 TO 135 MPH.
TREE DOWN AT DAILY AND MATHEWS ROADS.
MULTIPLE TREES DOWN AROUND MARCELLUS AND TWIN LAKES RD INTERSECTION.
MULTIPLE TREES DOWN AND BLOCKING ROAD ON M-62 AT TWIN LAKES RD.

80 BY 120 FOOT POLE BARN DESTROYED.

THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE CONFIRMED AN EF2 TORNADO IN SAINT JOSEPH COUNTY MICHIGAN. THE TORNADO BEGAN SW OF CONSTANTINE IN AN OPEN FIELD AND CONTINUED TO THE ESE WEAKENING TO AN EF0 TORNADO BEFORE ENDING NEAR FAWN RIVER RD AND STUBEY RD. THE TORNADO HAD A PATH LENGTH OF 9.4 MILES. DAMAGE INCLUDED TWO 350 FT CELL TOWERS BLOWN DOWN AND NUMEROUS TREES AND POWER POLES SNAPPED OR DAMAGED. SEVERAL STRUCTURES ALSO WERE DAMAGED. MAXIMUM WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 111 TO 135 MPH.

THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE CONFIRMED AND EF0 TORNADO IN WHITE COUNTY INDIANA. THE TORNADO BEGAN 3 MILES ENE OF CHALMERS AND CONTINUED TO THE ESE TO NEAR FREEMAN ROAD AND CHALMERS ROAD BEFORE EXITING INTO CARROLL COUNTY INDIANA. BUILDINGS...INCLUDING A CHURCH...AND OTHER STRUCTURES RECEIVED DAMAGED. THE TORNADO HAD A PATH LENGTH OF 3.75 MILES BEFORE EXITING INTO CARROLL COUNTY INDIANA. MAXIMUM WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 86 TO 110 MPH.
MULTIPLE STREETS IN MONTICELLO HAVE SEVERAL INCHES OF WATER ON THEM.

THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE CONFIRMED AN EF1 TORNADO IN CASS COUNTY INDIANA. THE TORNADO BEGAN IN CARROLL COUNTY INDIANA AND MOVED INTO CASS COUNTY INDIANA JUST SOUTH OF COUNTY ROAD E 700 N AND CONTINUED ESE ENDING TO THE EAST OF CR 300 E AND SOUTH OF CR 900 S OR TO THE SOUTHEAST OF THE CITY OF DEACON INDIANA. THE TORNADO HAD A PATH LENGTH OF 4.1 MILES. MAXIMUM WINDS WERE ESTIMATED AT 86 TO 110 MPH.
TRAILER FLIPPED POSSIBLE TORNADO
ALSO MEASURED A 68 MPH WIND GUST

THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE CONFIRMED AN EF3 TORNADO BEGINNING IN CASS COUNTY INDIANA AND CONTINUING INTO MIAMI COUNTY INDIANA. THE TORNADO BEGAN SOUTHWEST OF GRISSOM AIR FORCE BASE AND CONTINUED TO THE ESE ENDING TO THE EAST OF S 50 S BETWEEN E 900 S AND E 1000 S. ESTIMATED AT 136 TO 165 MPH.
ABOUT A 4 MILE DAMAGE PATH ACROSS SOUTHERN MIAMI COUNTY INCLUDES TWISTED TREES... BARNS AND HOMES DAMAGED... ONE HOME DESTROYED.
